Gibran is the third best-selling poet of all time, behind Shakespeare and Laozi.\", \"Bio from Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia. args.query: SELECT book_id, title, author, categories, details FROM books_info WHERE categories ILIKE '%Literature & Fiction%' AND (details ILIKE '%English%' OR details ILIKE '%Language%English%' OR details ILIKE '%"English"%' OR details ILIKE '%: English%');

args.query: SELECT purchase_id, AVG(rating) AS avg_rating, COUNT(*) AS review_count FROM review GROUP BY purchase_id HAVING AVG(rating)=5.0;

args.code:
import json, re, pandas as pd

# load books (possibly from file)
books_obj = var_call_cHZZsrYjPUdXxObh2bpObQk8
if isinstance(books_obj, str):
    with open(books_obj, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        books = json.load(f)
else:
    books = books_obj

perfect = var_call_ThHXe45m3dRfRo24qtut5tHv

df_books = pd.DataFrame(books)
df_perf = pd.DataFrame(perfect)

# normalize ids purchaseid_123 -> 123 ; bookid_123 -> 123
pat = re.compile(r'(\d+)')
df_books['id_num'] = df_books['book_id'].astype(str).str.extract(pat, expand=False)
df_perf['id_num'] = df_perf['purchase_id'].astype(str).str.extract(pat, expand=False)

# filter to literature & fiction already done; keep english already done
merged = df_books.merge(df_perf, on='id_num', how='inner')

# format output
merged['avg_rating'] = merged['avg_rating'].astype(float)
merged['review_count'] = merged['review_count'].astype(int)

out = merged[['title','author','book_id','avg_rating','review_count']].sort_values(['title','book_id']).to_dict(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(out, ensure_ascii=False))

args.code:
import json, pandas as pd
p = var_call_5vAnj61tKbKX9HUOZFME2ZRG
if isinstance(p, str):
    with open(p,'r',encoding='utf-8') as f:
        recs = json.load(f)
else:
    recs = p

df = pd.DataFrame(recs)
lines = []
for _, r in df.sort_values(['title','book_id']).iterrows():
    lines.append(f"{r['title']} | {r['book_id']} | avg_rating=5.0 | reviews={int(r['review_count'])}")
ans = "\n".join(lines)
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(ans, ensure_ascii=False))

        recs = json.load(f)
else:
    recs = p

df = pd.DataFrame(recs)
lines = []
for _, r in df.sort_values(['title','book_id']).iterrows():
    lines.append(f"{r['title']} | {r['book_id']} | avg_rating=5.0 | reviews={int(r['review_count'])}")
ans = "\\n".join(lines)
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(ans, ensure_ascii=False))
